item.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
item.description:
It took a while (relatively speaking) to get to this one. And, while this one has a lot of great individual scenes, I think it’s the weakest of the series.

This time around, we’re in the 70s, with the introduction of Beyoncé’s Foxxy Cleopatra. I enjoyed her role, especially her introduction, talking (so obviously) through a third party. She seems like she’s having fun and is a good counterpoint to Austin.

item.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
item.description:
The Harry Potter films have some problems1, but they came out just at the right time to be a large part of my growing up. I’ve seen them all and, while I think the books are better in a lot of ways, the movies have a lot of charm. Especially the first few.

They’re just done in such a magical way, really capturing the fantastic feel of the world. It makes you wish you could just hop right in and explore the world, Hogwarts castle especially.

item.title: Winnie the Pooh: A Very Merry Pooh Year
item.description:
It’s Winnie the Pooh–but now at Christmas time! Not sure how much else there is to say.

I so remember the old Winnie the Pooh & Christmas Too (from 1991). Man the nostalgia when the bugs (and their Napoleabug leader) sound the charge. I don’t even know when I would have watched it, but I remember that part!

It’s cute. The kids enjoyed it. It’s probably not something I’d ever choose to watch.

item.title: Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones
item.description:
Onward!

The things that I remember most about Attack of the Clones are the atmosphere of Kamino, the final battle on Genosis (mostly from the game Star Wars Battlefront), and some rather cringey burgeoning relationship dialog from Anakin and Padme.

I was not disappointed.
